tweets.full_text:
Tier S（これが最適解）4/n

・Sonnet 4.6 × 記事量産パイプライン（コスパ最強のメインエンジン）
・Sonnet 4.6 × Claude Codeの日常ドライバー（Opus級を4割安で）
・Opus 4.6 × 複数ファイルのリファクタ（設計判断が的確）
・Opus 4.6 × AI人格プロンプトの設計（深い文脈理解）
・Opus 4.6 × 事業戦略の壁打ち（思考の深さが別格）
・Opus 4.7 × 未知のコードベース解析（初見の読解力◎）
・Opus 4.7 × マルチステップのエージェント実行（途中放棄率60%減）
・Opus 4.7 × 複雑なデバッグ（競合状態・設計不整合の検出）
・Haiku 4.5 × 大量分類の自動処理（97トークン/秒）

Tier Sのポイントは「Opus 4.7の使いどころが限定されている」こと。4.7は全部に使うモデルではなく「他では解決できない複雑なタスク」に集中投下するモデル。逆にSonnet 4.6は「これ1本で8割のタスクを回せる」コスパの化け物。この2つの使い分けができている人が、今一番成果を出している。

Tier SS（バグ級） ↓↓

user.description:

https://t.co/rqHIQouPn3

tweets.full_text:
Tier A（ここから差がつく）3/n

・Sonnet 4.6 × SEO記事の本文執筆（Opus級の品質で4割安）
・Sonnet 4.6 × Claude Codeの日常コーディング（開発者の定番）
・Sonnet 4.6 × EC商品データの一括整理（速度と精度の両立）
・Sonnet 4.6 × プレスリリースの文案（実務レベル）
・Sonnet 4.6 × 多言語コンテンツの翻訳（精度◎）
・Opus 4.6 × 長文レポート・分析資料（深い構造化）
・Opus 4.6 × プロンプト設計の壁打ち（提案の質が高い）
・Opus 4.6 × SEO記事の構成設計（見出し設計が緻密）
・Opus 4.7 × スライド・文書の自動生成（視覚検証◎）
・Haiku 4.5 × APIの前段ルーター（コスト1/5で振り分け）

Tier Aから「どのモデルをどのタスクに当てるか」の判断力で成果が変わる。Sonnet 4.6がSEO記事の本文を書けるのにOpusを使っている人は、月のコストを4割捨てている計算になる。逆にOpus 4.6の構成設計をSonnetに任せると見出しの深さが落ちる。ここは使い分けの精度がそのまま成果の差になる。

Tier S（定番） ↓↓

user.name: Arck

tweets.full_text:
Everyone is laughing at Anthropic over the Claude Code leak. But what if we've just witnessed the most brilliant (and free) code review program in history? 🤯

The official version sounds ridiculous: they didn't keep an eye on the bundler, failed to set up CI/CD properly, and the .map files accidentally slipped into a public npm. Classic human error, nothing to see here.

But let's look at the results of this "mistake."

Over the past few days, thousands of top engineers from around the world dropped everything and dismantled the engine of the flagship AI agent down to the bolts for free. While juniors were giggling at the leaked system prompts (who even cares about prompts in 2026?), the serious folks started dissecting the architecture.

No corporation could ever buy this many hours of work from Senior developers and architects.

tweets.full_text:
this repo is turning any codebase into a memory layer for your coding agent

safishamsi/graphify

> 38,168 stars on github
> works across claude code, codex, cursor, openclaw, gemini cli, copilot cli, and more
> builds a knowledge graph from code, docs, screenshots, audio, and video
> claims 71.5x fewer tokens per query than rereading raw files
> supports clone any repo, merge graphs across repos, and persistent graph updates

this is basically graph rag for coding agents, but packaged like a skill instead of a research project

if your agent keeps forgetting why the codebase looks the way it does, this is the kind of repo worth stealing from

https://t.co/lt7HXipuJC

tweets.full_text: @aqibceo It’s true from my experience. Codex shat out garbage while Claude Code scaffolded an entire build with proper architecture, design tokens etc.

Claude Code v2.1.122 リリース

新機能まとめ：
・/resume にPRのURLで元セッションを検索
・/mcp で重複サーバーを自動検出
・Bedrockサービスティア設定が可能に

地味だけど使える更新

#ClaudeCode #個人開発

30以上のAI画像/動画モデルをMCP一本で呼べるHiggsfield MCPが出た

・Claude CodeからSeedance 2.0、Sora 2、Veo 3.1に直アクセス
・APIキー設定ゼロ。MCPサーバー追加だけで即利用
・画像は4K解像度、動画はシネマ品質で最大15秒
・キャラクターの一貫性を保つSoulトレーニングにも対応

解説はリプ欄↓

tweets.full_text: thank you for codex. it’s changed my life. I had never coded before i used it, and the app, skill implementation, and output is so good. it’s rare that i ever ask for changes. claude code can sometimes take twice as long on the same task. thanks for allowing people like me to experience the magic of programming. as a creative person it’s a whole new “creating something from nothing” experience.

user.name: skovie
user.description: decoding reality

tweets.full_text:
Pointed my Claude Code to @HeyGen's HyperFrames repo and had it build out a skill to create videos like this one direct from the app or CLI. Created it incredibly fast and the video itself didn't cost anything outside of the Claude usage.  

This was just a basic smoke test, so very little prompting or strategy went into the video itself, but plan on connecting this to some other skills we have made that will feed it with everything it needs to create strong DR video ads. 

Another unplanned benefit that came from this was the /gsap skill it installs significantly leveled up the animation capabilities of my landing page skill, so it can now do some really slick stuff when building out pages.

tweets.full_text:
Claude Code has features 95% of users never touch:

• hooks that auto-format, block dangerous bash & inject context
• skills that compound across every session
• subagent teams (explorer → planner → implementer → reviewer)
• in-process MCP servers you build yourself
• an Agent SDK that turns Claude into a pipeline step

12 levels. 30 battle-tested prompts. One guide.

https://t.co/em6vJqzV88

user.name: Liam Janssen
user.description: The podium belongs to builders. Deep in web3, deep in crypto markets, deep in F1 telemetry. Shipping while others are still researching.

tweets.full_text: @swapnakpanda 30 minutes gets you started. The skill curve sits inside the long sessions where you actually learn how to scope work for the agent and when to pull it back. Treating Claude Code like a junior engineer instead of a search box is the unlock.
